summary: Iteration 27 — Xino AI Blog Drafting + LinkedIn Auto-Publishing fully tested (backend + frontend). 16/16 backend tests pass after re-run (3 initial timeouts were transient ingress hiccups during back-to-back AI calls; isolated re-run passed). Frontend smoke verified for /app/blog (AI Suggest Topics modal + AI Generate + New post + LinkedIn icon per published row) and /app/linkedin (Connect card, queue with 6 items including blog-teaser + ai-native kinds, Approve/Publish-now/Skip/Delete actions, slot times Mon/Thu 04:30 UTC).

minor.issue: Long AI latency (20-90s via Claude) caused transient 20s timeouts when run back-to-back; passes on isolated re-run. Recommend client-side spinner ≥60s and not blocking other requests in the same browser tab.

critical_code_review_comments: linkedin_publisher.py:_used_slots() does a tz-aware/naive normalization that mutates the loop value but never persists — fine for runtime but tricky; consider always storing scheduled_for as tz-aware UTC at insert time (already done in add_to_queue + draft_from_blog, so safe).
linkedin_publisher.py:authorize endpoint stores oauth state in DB but never expires/cleans up. Recommend a TTL index on linkedin_oauth_states.created_at (e.g., 10 min) to prevent unbounded growth.
linkedin_publisher.py:_next_slot fallback returns 'after + 3 days' which can land on a non-Mon/Thu day. Edge case only (60-day window) but worth guarding.

blog_ai.py:_extract_json uses a greedy regex {[\s\S]*} that picks the OUTER braces — may include trailing prose if Claude returns extra text after the JSON. So far working; consider json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode for tighter parsing.
blog_ai.py: suggest_topics has no per-user rate limit; an admin could spam Claude. Add a simple in-memory or Mongo-backed rate limiter (e.g., 10/hr per user).

success_rate.backend: 100% (16/16 after isolated rerun of 3 transient timeouts)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ITER27 draft + published blog posts and several queue items during testing; queue items are best-effort cleaned in test_zz_cleanup. Note: draft-from-blog creates 2 items (blog-teaser + ai-native) that remain in 'queued' status — safe (never auto-published until 'approved').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth endpoint returns {token: ...} not {access_token: ...} — use j.get('token'). Onboarding intro modal blocks the login form on the public preview URL; bypass by calling /api/auth/login via fetch and setting localStorage.pj_token directly, then navigate to /app/*. LinkedIn OAuth flow cannot be completed (no real LinkedIn session) — only verify authorize_url construction + callback redirect-on-missing-code. The publishing engine (linkedin_scheduler_loop) runs every 60s and will attempt to publish APPROVED items whose scheduled_for is in the past — avoid approving any item whose slot is in the past, or delete it after creation. AI calls (Claude via emergentintegrations) can take 20-90s; use timeouts ≥120s.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. The 3 initial test timeouts were caused by 20s read-timeout being too tight when the previous AI draft call held the connection pool for ~90s; raising timeout or isolating AI tests resolves it.
